qa_pairs.question: What infrastructure renewal activities took place for Niagara and Mist starting January 6, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Beginning January 6, 2025, the Niagara cluster operated at approximately 50% capacity and Mist at approximately 35% capacity to support ongoing system improvements and the transition to the new Trillium system. Additional staged reductions were implemented in September 2025, leading to their eventual decommissioning.

qa_pairs.question: What were the initial capacity reductions for Niagara and Mist on January 6, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: On January 6, 2025, the Niagara cluster operated at approximately 50% capacity, and Mist operated at approximately 35% capacity.

qa_pairs.question: Why were the Niagara and Mist clusters operating at reduced capacity from January 6, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The Niagara and Mist clusters operated at reduced capacity to support ongoing system improvements and the transition to the new Trillium system.

qa_pairs.question: What happened to the Mist cluster on January 6, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The Mist cluster required a brief temporary shutdown on January 6, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: When was Niagara Open OnDemand decommissioned?
qa_pairs.answer: Niagara Open OnDemand was decommissioned on September 9, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What was the end-of-day capacity for Niagara on September 9, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The end-of-day capacity for Niagara on September 9, 2025, was 647 nodes.

qa_pairs.question: When was Trillium Open OnDemand launched?
qa_pairs.answer: Trillium Open OnDemand was launched on September 11, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What was the capacity of Niagara after the full-day maintenance on September 16, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: After the full-day maintenance on September 16, 2025, Niagara was reduced to 431 nodes.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Mist cluster decommissioned?
qa_pairs.answer: Mist was decommissioned on September 16, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Niagara cluster fully decommissioned?
qa_pairs.answer: Niagara was fully decommissioned on September 30, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: How many compute nodes was Niagara reduced to on September 4, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: On September 4, 2025, Niagara was reduced to 863 compute nodes.

qa_pairs.question: How many compute nodes was Niagara reduced to on September 24, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: On September 24, 2025, Niagara was reduced to 215 compute nodes.

qa_pairs.question: What caused a brief interruption on September 9, 2025, during the Niagara Open OnDemand decommissioning?
qa_pairs.answer: A data-centre firewall upgrade caused a brief interruption on September 9, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: When did the Graham compute cluster begin operating at reduced capacity?
qa_pairs.answer: The Graham compute cluster began operating at reduced capacity on February 25, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What was the capacity of the Graham cluster during its reduction period starting February 25, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The Graham compute cluster operated at approximately 25% capacity starting February 25, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: Why was the Graham cluster operating at reduced capacity?
qa_pairs.answer: Graham operated at reduced capacity due to data migration and system transition work in preparation for its retirement.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Graham cluster fully decommissioned?
qa_pairs.answer: Graham was fully decommissioned on September 1, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: Was Graham Cloud operational during the reduction period?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Graham Cloud remained fully operational throughout the reduction period.

qa_pairs.question: Were user logins and storage accessible on Graham during the reduction?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, user logins remained available and storage was accessible, though project storage was temporarily read-only during migration.

qa_pairs.question: What was the status of project storage on Graham during the reduction period?
qa_pairs.answer: Project storage on Graham was temporarily read-only during migration.

qa_pairs.question: Which GPU job types did Graham support during its simplified scheduling configuration?
qa_pairs.answer: During its simplified scheduling configuration, Graham supported CPU and GPU jobs including V100, T4, A100, and A5000.

qa_pairs.question: Why was the start of Graham's reduction postponed?
qa_pairs.answer: The start of Graham's reduction was postponed from January to February while storage migration progressed.

qa_pairs.question: What was unavailable from July 15 to August 25, 2025, for Béluga, Narval, and Juno?
qa_pairs.answer: From July 15 to August 25, 2025, the tape storage system behind the TSM service, including backups and migrated /nearline data, was unavailable.

qa_pairs.question: What was the reason for the tape storage system unavailability for Béluga, Narval, and Juno?
qa_pairs.answer: The tape storage system was unavailable due to its migration to a new data centre.

qa_pairs.question: Were file backup and restore services available during the tape storage outage from July 15 to August 25, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: No, file backup and restore services were unavailable during this period.

qa_pairs.question: What happened to data created or modified on the tape system between July 15 and August 11, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Data created or modified on the tape system between July 15 and August 11, 2025, could not be recovered.

qa_pairs.question: Were /nearline files migrated to tape accessible on Béluga and Narval during the outage?
qa_pairs.answer: No, files in /nearline that had been migrated to tape were not accessible on Béluga and Narval during the outage.

qa_pairs.question: Was the TSM service available during the period from July 15 to August 25, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the TSM service itself was fully unavailable during this period.

qa_pairs.question: Did the tape storage system outage affect other storage systems or compute nodes on Béluga, Narval, and Juno?
qa_pairs.answer: No, other storage systems, compute nodes on all clusters, and Juno Cloud instances remained fully operational during the tape storage system outage.

qa_pairs.question: Did Globus transfers function normally during the tape storage outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Globus transfers functioned normally except when accessing tape-migrated /nearline files.

qa_pairs.question: When were some compute nodes on Fir temporarily unavailable?
qa_pairs.answer: Some compute nodes on Fir were temporarily unavailable from Monday, August 25, through Thursday, August 28, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What was the reason for the reduction in Fir's capacity from August 25 to September 5, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The reduction in Fir's capacity was to support the commissioning of new cooling equipment.

qa_pairs.question: What impact did the Fir reduction have on job execution?
qa_pairs.answer: During the Fir reduction, fewer jobs ran concurrently, jobs often started more slowly, and wait times were longer.

qa_pairs.question: How long was Fir operating at reduced capacity?
qa_pairs.answer: Fir operated at reduced capacity from August 25 through September 5, 2025, a total of 12 days.

qa_pairs.question: What was the duration of the initial capacity reduction for Niagara and Mist starting January 6, 2025, as noted in the T:302 entry?
qa_pairs.answer: The initial capacity reduction for Niagara and Mist, starting January 6, 2025, lasted 254 days, until September 16, 2025, as noted in the T:302 entry.
